Welcome — pull up a chair.

A Story

How Boundless Summits Began

Years ago, on a morning run, I spotted another runner ahead of me on the trail. Without thinking, I made it my mission to catch him. I locked in, picked up my pace, and pushed harder mile after mile — until I finally pulled ahead.

And then I looked up.

I had no idea where I was. In my determination to outrun someone else, I had completely abandoned my own route. I had won a race that wasn't mine — and lost the one that was.

That moment changed everything for me. And I see the same pattern in professionals more often than not — chasing titles, comparing themselves to peers, copying someone else's career playbook instead of writing their own.

Boundless Summits exists to support you in rewriting the script — with intention, self-reflection, and action that align with who you really are.

Your race. Your summits. Your way.

What to Expect

Here's what you can expect from me.

I will listen without judgment, but with intent.

I will stay curious, hold space, and trust you to lead the conversation. The work that needs to happen is yours — and you are fully capable of it. My role is to walk alongside you while you find your summit.

Coaching Philosophy

What I Believe About Coaching

The answers are already within you.

You carry the resilience, the lived experience, and the wisdom needed to navigate whatever you're facing. You may not see it clearly right now — but it's there.

The journey is yours. The direction is yours. The path is yours.

My role isn't to lead you somewhere. It's to walk alongside you, asking questions that support your discovery. You hold the choices. You are responsible for your own path.

Coaching builds awareness — of your strengths, your patterns, and the possibilities you haven't yet explored.

From that awareness, you generate your own ideas, decide what you're willing to act on, and reframe what once felt fixed. The shift happens in you — not from me.

You may have heard the words coaching, counseling, consulting, and mentoring used interchangeably. They're not the same. Read about my approach →

Working Together

Working together looks different for everyone.

Explore the ways we might work together — through one-on-one coaching, group programs, or something tailored to where you are.
